Operation 'checkInParticipant' has semantic quality issues: vague_description, missing_context, missing_input_details, missing_output_details, missing_side_effects, poor_ai_interpretability. The current summary and description are identical and extremely vague, providing no actionable information for AI agents. The improved version clarifies: (1) the specific action being performed (marking as checked in), (2) required inputs (event ID and participant ID), (3) the side effect (updates check-in status and records attendance), (4) the response type (CheckInResponse), and (5) the typical use case (event entry verification). This helps AI agents understand when and how to use this operation.

data.summary_suggestion: Mark participant as checked in for event
data.description_suggestion: Marks a participant as checked in for a specific event. Requires the event ID and participant ID as path parameters. This operation records the participant's attendance and updates their check-in status. Returns a CheckInResponse object containing the updated check-in information. This action is typically used at event entry points to verify and record participant attendance.
data.reasoning: The current summary and description are identical and extremely vague, providing no actionable information for AI agents. The improved version clarifies: (1) the specific action being performed (marking as checked in), (2) required inputs (event ID and participant ID), (3) the side effect (updates check-in status and records attendance), (4) the response type (CheckInResponse), and (5) the typical use case (event entry verification). This helps AI agents understand when and how to use this operation.
